title: Simple Play Kitchen Narrow Fridge
summary: A narrow toy fridge-freezer cabinet — a plain plywood-backed box with a shelf, split into a small top freezer and a taller fridge compartment, each with its own hinged door.
description:
This is a plain box build: two sides, three top/bottom/divider panels, and a fridge shelf, nailed and glued together with finish nails and a pneumatic nailer (or a hammer and care, if that's what's on hand). The plywood back gets nailed on next — **nail into the shelf and divider edges too, not just the two sides**, since that's what keeps a narrow cabinet like this from racking when a small hand yanks a door open.

The two doors — a short freezer door up top and a taller fridge door below — are cut to the plan's dimensions but **may need final trimming to fit your actual opening**, since a fraction of an inch of variance in the box assembly changes the door gap. Euro-style inset hinges are a good fit here: concealed, easy to mount, and inexpensive, and they work equally well for full-overlay doors if that's the look you want instead. Add magnetic clasps to keep the doors shut and finish with handles sized for small hands.

This fridge pairs with this catalog's play kitchen sink-and-stove cabinet as a matching set, but it's a complete standalone build on its own.

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Assemble the sides, top, bottom, and divider with finish nails and glue.

steps.title: Add the bottom trim and fridge shelf
steps.body: Attach the bottom trim and set the fridge shelf in place inside the taller compartment.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Nail the plywood back to the box, nailing into the shelf and divider edges as well as the sides.

steps.title: Add the doors
steps.body: Trim the freezer and fridge doors to fit their openings if needed, then hang with euro-style inset hinges. Add magnetic clasps and handles.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand and paint the whole cabinet.

unresolvedImages.alt: Simple play kitchen narrow fridge with two doors
